title: linaria elatine subsp. sieberi
intro: Linaria elatine subsp. sieberi, also known as Sieber's Toadflax or Sieber's Linaria, is a perennial flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Linaria elatine subsp. sieberi include relief from respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate digestive problems. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in folk medicine to treat various ailments.
